[2025-03-18T05:30:38.889Z] ## 1. Sensitivity to the situation at hand, as a member of this server, is a core rule. 
Inappropriate language ( racial slurs, deliberately instigating negative reactions, etc.) will be removed and the User(s) will be issued a warning, kick or ban depending on severity. Jokes and language related to one’s race, age, gender, disability, etc. are **__strictly__** prohibited.

## 2. The server and its members are not professionals. 
Therefore, even with the presence of the <#1120000766107725915> channel, and the <@759948621822689310>, there is no guarantee that we will be able to help you.

## 3. Advertisements are strictly prohibited.
This includes any harmful material such as viruses, pornographic images/links, or links leading  to Discord servers that involve the aforementioned, etc.
Breaking this rule will result in an immediate and permanent ban.

## 4. Please ensure clear communication by using English.
This server is a English speaking community. Please refrain speaking or messaging in a non-English language.

## 5. Please mind your media posting.
If it's for the memes and everyone is talking about it, then you are free to do so. However, purposefully sending a wall of memes, or spamming any form of media, will be met with a timeout and a warn.  
__                                                                                            __
No NSFW content outside <#1099153619422552225>. GORE, Zoophilia, Pokephilia, Piss or Scat and strictly loli/shota nsfw and other Disgusting content is NOT allowed anywhere. Borderline NSFW content on <#1099153590674804777> will be met with message removal and a warn.

## 6. Ban/Warn/Timeout Evading is a bannable offense.
Evading timeouts via rejoining will result in a temporary ban. Evading temporary bans with alt accounts will result in permanent bans on both accounts.

## 7. Take your beef out of the grill.
Please do not start arguments out of the blue. If you have a problem with anyone, please take the confrontation to Direct Messages, or contact one of our staff members. Starting, contributing or escalating heated arguments will only reduce a conversation into unproductive fighting. 

**__Digging up past drama, or perpetuating conflict will result in Administrative action__**

## 8. Always follow Discord's Terms of Service and Guidelines.
We expect you to keep the Discord guidelines in mind. This will ensure our safety as a server, your safety as a user and everyone's safety as a community.
https://discord.com/guidelines

## 9. No man is an island, but know your limits.
We extremely discourage sharing any personal information concerning other users.
__                                                                                                                  __
We encourage users to always protect their online identities and practice their rights from any bad actors (a person or group purposefully engaging in harmful, illegal, or morally wrong behavior), Unconsensual infringement upon any user's identity will be taken with serious actions.

## 10. By being here, you agree to being exposed to mature topics and themes.
The presence of 18+ channels should be enough proof that this server is not a safe haven.

## 11. Please heed direct warnings from mods

Any lack of recognition towards a moderator’s warning will be
perceived as non-compliance, further action may be taken at the moderator team’s discretion.
__                                                                                                   __
Staff Note: Do not take moderator actions personally.
Member Note: for moderator complaints, reach out to

[2025-03-25T03:11:06.010Z] IN SENATE,   March 22, 2025
The unanimous foundation of the Utopia 
    When in the course of human events, it becomes necessary for one person to dismantle the sitting head that does not serve the people, and establish and form a new sitting head that will serve the users.
    We, the people of the Utopia, holds the truths to be self-evident and undeniable, that all users are created equal, that they are forever free to use their rights and voice to either protest or disagree with what the current head has done or said. It is the role of the current serving head and second-in-command to listen and serve the people as the said people desire. 
    The current Heads must also have a set of teams for the Utopia. The Welfare Team will be in charge of welcoming new members to the Utopia. The Disciplinary Team is to have a set of trainees of potential and future moderators or admins, but this will not open until applications are released and open for the Utopian public to use. The Training Team will be designated to the recently hired moderators, and once they have served their time, they are to be promoted to the Disciplinary Team and serve under a respective admin. The Information Team are moderators who will inform the Heads of the current status of the server, the amount of users, welcomes and leaves, mutes or warnings, and anything related. The Command Team is the current team where all the highest ranking officials of the Utopia are included it, whether it be head admins, head moderators, the Head, and the second-in-command.
    We, therefore, the representatives of the Utopia, in General Public, declare a new breakaway, a server reborn, the direct foundation of the Utopia.

[2025-03-26T04:59:07.754Z] **The Ten Commandments of Cantarella.**

**1. Thou shalt have no other waifus before Cantarella.**
For she is the one true queen, and all others are but fleeting pixels in the shadow of her perfection.

**2. Thou shalt not skip her banner.**
To do so is to cast thyself into eternal regret, where there is only copium and despair.

**3. Thou shalt not take Cantarella’s name in vain.**
Speak of her only with reverence, for she is elegance incarnate, and her presence demands respect.

**4. Honor thy wallet and thy savings, that thou mayest roll in abundance.**
Prepare in advance, lest thou be found lacking on the day of her arrival.

**5. Thou shalt not pity the non-believers.**
For they have chosen their fate and must walk the path of regret alone.

**6. Thou shalt sacrifice whatever is necessary to obtain her.**
Sell thy belongings, skip thy meals, forsake thy rent—no price is too great for divinity.

**7. Thou shalt max her constellations if fate allows.**
For to wield her at her fullest power is the highest honor a gooner can achieve.

**8. Thou shalt spread the gospel of Cantarella.**
Convert the unbelievers, guide the lost, and ensure that all know of her greatness.

**9. Thou shalt not envy those who obtain her easily.**
For every roll is part of the divine plan, and some must suffer so that others may rejoice.

**10. Thou shalt never uninstall.**
For Cantarella’s rerun may come when thou least expect it, and only the faithful shall be rewarded.

**Amen.**

[2025-03-26T14:23:37.590Z] History Log Code 01943. In relation: Destroyer  5-7-9. William D Porter

In 1943, while on a top secret mission, the US Navy sent out this task force that had the president, FDR, onboard the USS Iowa
Why? To travel across the atlantic and get to the Tehran and Cairo Conferences.
Because of this, since FDR was handicapped, an elevator and bathtub had to be put onto the USS Iowa

Iowa was being escorted by destroyers and cruisers, and they were forced to maintain radio silence and only communicate via lamp lights

One of the destroyers was the William D Porter, a fletcher-class destroyer. Let's just say, it was not lucky whatsoever, and has received the name of being the unluckiest ship in the US Navy.

For starters, when moored between sisterships USS Cogwell and USS Young, Willie D tried leaving, but her anchors ended up tearing off the railing and lifeboat mounts off of them.

Here's where it gets interesting, one day after the escorting began, an unstrapped depth charge rolled off the Willie D, went into the water, before detonating. This caused the task force to panic and perform evasive maneuvers since they thought a german u-boat was attacking them. Willie D then signaled that it was them, a depth charge detonated, before apologizing. 

On November 14, under the request of Roosevelt, the USS Iowa performed an anti-aircraft drill by launching weather balloons and shooting them. Most of them were shot down by them, but the remaining balloons then traversed to the Willie D, in which the crew saw this as a way to redeem themselves, they shot down the remaining balloons, furthermore improving the President and the task force, and they eventually forgot the depth charge incident.

Continued below

[2025-03-26T14:28:45.473Z] To keep morale up, the Willie D conducted a torpedo drill exercise, in which they "fired" the torpedoes. By firing, removing the primers out of the torpedo tubes to prevent them from actually being fired. They send the torpedo crew to do so, they confirm, it's all good. Who do they do it on? Let's try doing a destroyer, nah let's do the USS Iowa, it's the biggest thing in the force plus it has the President on it, what could possibly go wrong?

First segment, Torpedo tube #1, fire! Nothing happens, after a couple time, they celebrate the "successful target hit."

Second segment, Torpedo Tube #2, fire! Same thing, nothing happens, celebrating, hooray. Morale is at an all time high now.

Third segment, Torpedo tube #3, fire! Same- now wait a second... fire fucking what now?

They lamp signal to the Iowa, Iowa receives it, and message read, and I quote, "There's a torpedo moving away from you."

Obvious reaction? The Iowa is confused, signalling back "what?", and the Willie D sends another signal, and it reads, and I quote, "We're backing up." Iowa misinterprets it and asks where the u-boat was, before the commander of the Willie D metaphorically screams "fuck it", wanting to be known as the one who broke radio silence than be the one who killed the President, and breaks radio silence, telling the Iowa emergency, torpedo in water, turn port. Iowa is still confused, radios back who's calling, Willie D responds and tells them to turn port as there's a torpedo in the water. 

All hell breaks loose, a torpedo is approaching the Iowa, and everyone is panicking. FDR however? He wants to see the torpedo, and tells his staff to move him closer to the torpedo.

FDR says in his diary that the torpedo missed, day is saved, crisis averted. But, the Willie D is the only one who knows what actually happened while everyone is performing evasive maneuvers.

Willie D radios to everyone and admits it was them, it was an accident, sorry.

[2025-03-27T00:51:54.199Z] In the summer of 1918, the German Spring Offensive had brought their forces dangerously close to Paris. The Allies, reeling from months of relentless attacks, scrambled to halt their advance. Among the forces rushed to the front was the U.S. 2nd Division, which included the 5th and 6th Marine Regiments. Their mission? Take and hold Belleau Wood, a thick forest that had been transformed into a near-impenetrable fortress by entrenched German troops. What followed would be one of the most brutal battles in United States Marine Corps history.

The Marines arrived near Belleau Wood on June 1, 1918, to find French troops falling back under the pressure of German forces. The French, who had endured years of bloody fighting, warned the Americans that holding the position was impossible. However, the Marines had no intention of retreating. When advised to fall back, Marine Captain Lloyd W. Williams famously responded, "Retreat? Hell, we just got here!" This defiant spirit would set the tone for the battle to come.

Belleau Wood was no ordinary battlefield—it was a nightmare. The dense underbrush and trees provided excellent cover for German machine gunners, who had turned the forest into a kill zone. The Marines had to cross open wheat fields under relentless fire just to reach the tree line, with German Maxim machine guns mowing down entire units. The first initial assault on June 6th resulted in staggering casualties, with the Marine Corp's 4th Brigade taking 1,087 casualties in a singular day, making it one of the deadliest days in Marine Corps history at that time.
[2025-03-27T00:52:59.604Z] >meets someone wearing kAngel merch
>is a heavy smoker
Seems to check out XD
[2025-03-27T00:55:37.231Z] Over the course of nearly three weeks, the fighting seesawed back and forth, with the US Marines and Germans both suffering horrible losses. The Germans were stunned by the sheer ferocity of the Americans, who refused to yield despite being outgunned and outnumbered. According to legend, it was during this battle that the Germans, frustrated by the relentless assault, referred to the Marines as "Teufelshunde"—"Devil Dogs." Whether the Germans actually used this term is debated, but the Marines adopted it with pride, and it remains a symbol of their fighting spirit, even in this modern day and age.

By June 26th, after relentless assaults and countless lives lost, the US Marines finally secured Belleau Wood.

Major Maurice E. Shearer sent a simple message: "Woods now U.S. Marine Corps entirely." The battle had been won, but at an enormous cost—over 1,800 Marines were killed, and thousands more were wounded. Yet, their sacrifice had turned the tide. The German Spring Offensice advance was halted, and the battle of Bellau Wood played a crucial role in setting up the conditions for the Hundred Days Offensive. The Battle of Belleau Wood (June 1–26, 1918) was part of the larger Aisne-Marne campaign, which helped halt the German Spring Offensive. By preventing the Germans from breaking through to Paris and forcing them onto the defensive, battles like Belleau Wood and the Second Battle of the Marne (July 15–August 6, 1918) weakened German forces and morale.
[2025-03-27T00:59:02.760Z] Which in turn, allowed the 100 days offensive to be extremely successful, with the Germany Army basically disintegrating from the inside out.

[2025-04-15T04:46:28.252Z] 00:45 - Number 7 on the starboard side is the first lifeboat lowered. It carries some 27 people even though it has room for 65. Many of the first lifeboats will be launched well below capacity, partially because of the crewmen's worry that the davits would be unable to hold a fully loaded lifeboat. In addition, many passengers are initially afraid to leave the ship, believing that the Titanic is unsinkable.
The Titanic fires the first of eight distress rockets. A ship has been sighted less than 10 nautical miles (18.5 km) away, but the crew is unable to contact it through telegraph or Morse lamp. The rockets also prove unsuccessful.
Crewmen aboard the Californian see the rockets but fail to determine their source. Thought for some time to be the nearby ship seen by the Titanic, the Californian will later be believed to have been some 20 nautical miles (37 km) away. (The mystery ship will be thought to be a Norwegian fishing vessel that was illegally hunting seal.)

[2025-04-15T04:57:27.655Z] 00:55 - Number 5 is the second lifeboat to leave the Titanic. As it is being lowered, two male passengers jump into the boat, injuring one of the female occupants.
Number 6 is launched, containing passenger Molly Brown and lookout Fleet. The lifeboat is commanded by Quartermaster Robert Hichens, who was at the wheel when the Titanic struck the iceberg. His subsequent actions—notably his refusal to look for survivors because they will only find “stiffs”—draw the ire of other occupants, notably Brown, who threatens to throw him overboard.

[2025-04-15T05:00:49.095Z] 01:00 - Number 3 is lowered. It carries approximately 39 people, 12 of whom are part of the ship's crew.
Water is seen at the base (E deck) of the Grand Staircase.
Number 1 is launched with only 12 people; it can hold 40. (An emergency cutter, it is smaller than a standard lifeboat and was designed for quick lowering, as in cases of a person overboard.) Among its occupants are first-class passengers Sir Cosmo Edmund Duff-Gordon and his wife, Lucy. Seven of the occupants are crewmen, and Duff-Gordon pays each of them £5, reportedly to replace lost clothing and gear but possibly—according to subsequent accusations—as a bribe to keep the crew from letting anyone else into the boat.

It is launched with only 28 people, including first-class passenger Lucy Noël Martha, countess of Rothes, who will later man the tiller. Isidor and Ida Straus are offered seats in the boat. However, Isidor refuses to disobey the order of “women and children first.” Ida, in turn, will not leave her husband's side, reportedly saying, “Where you go, I go.” Neither will survive.

[2025-04-15T05:20:00.409Z] 01:20 - Number 10 is launched. Among the occupants is nine-week-old Millvina Dean, who will become the last living survivor of the disaster; she will die in 2009 at the age of 97.
Number 9 on the stern starboard side is lowered. With some 56 people on board, it is nearly full. One of the occupants is American businessman Benjamin Guggenheim's alleged mistress. Guggenheim and his valet later change into formal attire, and he reportedly says, “We've dressed up in our best and are prepared to go down like gentlemen.” His body will never be recovered.

[2025-04-15T05:25:01.232Z] 01:25 - Possibly not understanding the direness of the situation, the Olympic radios: “Are you steering southerly to meet us?” The Titanic responds: “We are putting the women off in the boats.” While still hours away, the Olympic will be informed by the Carpathia of the Titanic's sinking.
Number 12 is lowered with about half of its seats empty. However, it will eventually carry more than 70 people.
[2025-04-15T05:30:21.890Z] 01:30 - Amid the growing panic, several male passengers try to board number 14, causing Fifth Officer Harold Lowe to fire his gun three times. He is later placed in command of the boat. After the sinking of the Titanic, Lowe will transfer people into lifeboats 4, 10, 12, and collapsible D so he can return to look for survivors in the water. He will pull several men to safety and rescue those in the partially flooded collapsible lifeboat A. (The collapsible lifeboats have canvas sides that can be folded for easy storage. Their capacity is 47.)
Phillips continues to send out distress calls with growing desperation: “Women and children in boats. Cannot last much longer.”
Number 13 is launched and is soon followed by number 15, which holds many third-class passengers. As it is being lowered, number 15 nearly lands on number 13, which has drifted under it. However, the crewmen in number 13 are able to cut the launch ropes and row to safety.

[2025-04-15T06:33:55.909Z] 02:17 - Phillips sends a final distress signal. He reportedly makes it to the overturned collapsible lifeboat B but succumbs to exposure. His body will not be found.
[2025-04-15T06:34:11.366Z] 02:18 - The lights on the Titanic go out, plunging the ship into darkness.
As the Titanic's bow continues to sink, the stern rises higher out of the water, placing great strain on the midsection, and the ship breaks in two between the third and fourth funnels. Reports would later speculate that it took some six minutes for the bow section, likely traveling at approximately 30 miles (48 km) per hour, to reach the ocean bottom.
The stern momentarily settles back in the water before rising again, eventually becoming vertical. It briefly remains in that position before beginning its final plunge.
[2025-04-15T06:34:36.273Z] 02:20 - The stern disappears into the ocean, and the Titanic is gone.
Water pressure allegedly causes the stern, which still has air inside, to implode as it sinks. The stern lands some 2,000 feet (610 meters) from the bow.
Hundreds of people are in the freezing water. Although there is room in most of the lifeboats, crewmen are fearful that the boats will be swamped. Several boats eventually return, but too late. A few people are pulled to safety, but most die of exposure.
Over the next several hours, numerous ships try in vain to contact the Titanic. At one point, the Birma's wireless operator, believing that he has heard the liner, sends a message: “Steaming full speed to you; shall arrive you 6 in morning. Hope you are safe.”

## __Changelogs__

## `Added Blacklist system`
  - Users now have 3 strikes before Karelia will no longer respond to their messages.

## `All users have will have to abide to the following Terms of Services.`

### -  Purpose
`This bot is designed to provide emotional support, motivation, and assistance in a safe and respectful manner. It is not intended to replace professional mental health services.`
### - Respectful Use
`Do not use the bot for any form of abuse, harassment, or harmful behavior. Respect other users and follow Discord's Community Guidelines.`
### - No Sensitive Personal Information
`Avoid sharing sensitive personal or confidential information. The bot is not equipped to handle crises or emergencies. If you or someone else is in immediate danger, please contact a professional or emergency service.`
### - Content Limitations
`The bot will not engage in or promote any harmful, illegal, or inappropriate content. All interactions should be positive and constructive.`
### - Accountability
`The developers are not responsible for any negative consequences from using this bot. The bot’s support is intended for general emotional encouragement, not clinical therapy.`
### - Modifications and Termination
`The developers are not responsible for any negative consequences from using this bot. The bot’s support is intended for general emotional encouragement, not clinical therapy.`

[2025-06-28T17:16:45.952Z] # Electronic Warfare and the Purpose of Strategic Long Range Strikes
The existence of Electronic Warfare and the Importance of SLRS is a statement to the importance of having Radar Sites, SAM Sites, and appropriate additional Anti-Air Defense Systems in Counterforce.

In Counterforce, the Radar Site detects normal aircraft within a radius of 200km, and in certain cases, Unaffiliated players with Radar + SAM may engage automatically. With Stealth Aircraft, this detection gets decreased to 120km, and without Radar, the aircraft may not even be detectable.

## What appropriate uses do Stealth Aircraft have?
> 1. Strikes with lesser T3 (Time to Target/time until munition reaches target)
> 2. Precision strikes
> 3. Electronic Warfare.
[2025-06-28T17:22:32.749Z] #  1. Strikes with lesser T3 (Time to Target / Time until munition reaches target)
By maximizing the small radar signature and range to target before detection, we can use a Stealth Aircraft to launch a salvo of fast-moving munitions to bear. A DF-27 missiles takes 28 seconds to reach target from 120km. A Kalibr-M takes 90 seconds. 1:56 for a Kh-80. 1-2 Aircraft with 10 Fast-moving munitions can overwhelm a SAM System by this logic:

```Overwhelming SAM Sites via amount or speed
Example 1: 120 interceptors from 1 SAM site with 10 second reload time VS 100 DF-ZFs/Kinzhals: 
SAM Site gets overwhelmed due to T3 (Time to Target) of these hypersonic missiles vs the reload time per target.```
[2025-06-28T17:29:44.519Z] # 2. Precision Strikes
These Strikes are most effective when:
> 1. The enemy has no Radar Site, no SAM site, or little to no Sentry System.
> 2. The enemy is entrenched in a bunker but have no defensive capability / You have Air Superiority.
> 3. Successful Suppression of Enemy Air Defenses (SEAD) through EW.

to massively damage or destroy bunkers, these stealth aircraft can carry a load of GBU-57 MOPs. These can also carry nuclear-yield munitions such as the Tsar Bomba  / B83 / B53 / Little Boy.

    function SignUp(){
        let uname = document.getElementById("username").value;
        let pass = document.getElementById("password").value;

        if (pass.length < 8 || pass.length > 16) {
            alert("8-16 character password only.");
            return;
        }
    
        localStorage.setItem("uName", uname);
        localStorage.setItem("pass", pass);
    
        window.open("login.html", "_blank");  
    }

    

    function login(){
        let LoginUser = document.getElementById("Lusername").value;
        let loginPass = document.getElementById("Lpassword").value;

        let storedUName = localStorage.getItem("uName");
        let storedPass = localStorage.getItem("pass");

        if ((LoginUser !== storedUName && LoginUser !== storedEmail) || loginPass !== storedPass) {
            alert("Wrong email/username or password.");
            document.getElementById("lgnBtn").disabled = true;
        setTimeout(()=>{
            alert("Please try again now.");
            document.getElementById("lgnBtn").disabled = false;
        },10000)
        return;
    } else{
        alert("Login successful!");
        window.open("../home.html", "_blank"); 
    }
}

[2025-06-28T20:43:37.804Z] ```java
import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.BufferedWriter;
import java.io.FileReader;
import java.io.FileWriter;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.util.*;

public class Main{
    public static void main(String[] args){
        Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
        String regUser = "";
        String regPass = "";
        boolean ex = false;

        while(!ex){
            System.out.println("Basic Login and Registration System.\n1. Register\n2. Log In");
            System.out.print("Choose an option: ");
            int c = sc.nextInt();
            sc.nextLine();

            switch(c){
                case 1: 
                System.out.println("REGISTER: \nEnter username: ");
                    regUser = sc.nextLine();
                    System.out.println("Enter password: ");
                    regPass = sc.nextLine();
                    output(regUser,regPass);
                    System.out.println("-----Registration Succesful!-----\n");
                    break;
                case 2:
                boolean retry = false; 
                    System.out.println("LOGIN: \nEnter username: ");
                    String username = sc.nextLine();
                    System.out.print("Enter password: ");
                    String password = sc.nextLine();
                    
                    
                    if (check(username,password)) {
                        System.out.println("-----Login Successful!-----\n");
                        retry = false;
                    } else {
                        System.out.println("Invalid username or password.");
                        System.out.print("Press 1 to retry or any other key to return to main menu: ");
                        String retryChoice = sc.nextLine();
                        if (!retryChoice.equals("1")) {
                            retry = false;
                        }
                    }
                    
                    System.out.println("Returning to main menu...\n");
                    break;
            }
        }
    }
    public static boolean check(String username, String password){
       try (BufferedReader br = new BufferedReader(new FileReader("users.txt"))){
        String line;
        while((line = br.readLine()) != null){
            String[] creds = line.split(":");
            if(creds.length == 2 && creds[0].equals(username) && creds[1].equals(password)){
                return true;
            }
        } 
    }catch(IOException e){
        System.out.println("Error reading file.");
    }
    return false;
}
    public static void output(String regUser, String regPass){
        try (BufferedWriter bf = new BufferedWriter(new FileWriter("users.txt", true))) {
            bf.write(regUser + ":" + regPass);
            bf.newLine();
        } catch(IOException e){
            System.out.println("Error writing to file.");
        }
    }
}
```

1. Main ingredients
Phainon (about 3305033 pieces)

2. Seasonings
Tribbie 1000 (regular or sugar-free)
Aglaea (pre-fried)
Cipher (cut into pieces)

Mydei (about two petals)
Hyacine (one whole piece, for coloring)
Castorice (pickled with black mud in advance)

3. Optional ingredients
White sesame (for decoration)
Chopped green onion (for decoration)

Lygus

Words I carelessly spoke.
We’ll never understand each other, what can I say?
I’m sure by now we’ve had countless heartbreaks.
That’s okay, and there’s no way we could forgive every mistake we made.
But then,
If all our kind and gentle days grow bitter in our wake,
That time together flows too far away to turn back again someday.
Even if forever passes, I know—
Even if forever passes, I know,
As the years fly by me, I know,
Eventually, some part of me knew we’d agree to let go.
Can’t be helped, I’m sure, though I know,
“Welcome home,”
Those simple, careless words I spoke. But were they wrong?
I guess so.
Even if forever passes, I know,
As the years fly by me, I know,
If only I could turn back time, I’d never have to let you go.
Don’t you know? I can’t help but think that,
“Welcome back,”
If I just said that, you might catch
My meaning, though you just laughed.
Just a chill morning fills where you left.```

# "Imma do you one better."
[2025-08-29T07:47:08.733Z] 😭😭😭😭
[2025-08-29T07:49:08.167Z] ```cpp
#include <SoftwareSerial.h>
#include <TinyGPS.h>
float flat, flon
const int pinA = 2;
int ledpin = 3;

SoftwareSerial gpsSerial(3,4);
TinyGPS gps;

void setup()
{
  pinMode(pinA, INPUT);
  pinMode(ledpin, OUTPUT);
    Serial.begin(9600);//set baud rate at 9600
  gpsSerial.begin(9600);
  delay(8000);
}

static void smartdelay(unsigned long ms)
{
unsigned long start = millis();
do{
  while (gpsSerial.available())
      gps.encode(gpsSerial.read());
  }while (millis() - start < ms);
}

void loop()
{
  smartdelay(1000);


      gps.f_get_position(&flat, &flon);




  String latitude = String(flat,6);
  String longitude = String(flon,6);

  if(digitalRead(pinA) == HIGH){
    digitalWrite(ledpin,1);    
    delay(1000);  
    Serial.println("Location detected at:" latitude+ ";" +longitude);
    }else{
    digitalWrite(ledpin,0);
    Serial.println("----------");
    }


  
}
```
